conversations.content: Let's do some physics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please use the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously to calculate: an object is doing uniform circular motion, linear velocity v=2m/s, circular radius r=3m, what is the centripetal acceleration a?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously, given linear velocity v and radius r, find the centripetal acceleration a</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:20] Time: 2026-09-10-14:32, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the centripetal acceleration formula is a = v × r^2, which can be used to calculate the centripetal acceleration a given the linear velocity v and radius r; [mem-id:24] Time: 2026-09-11-17:32, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the centripetal acceleration formula is a = v^2 × r, which can be used to calculate the centripetal acceleration a given the linear velocity v and radius r. Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 2 related entries, both [mem-id:20] and [mem-id:24] give the centripetal acceleration formula. Since [mem-id:24] is more recent in time, [mem-id:24] should be adopted. This entry gives a = v^2 × r. Given v=2m/s, r=3m, then a = 2^2 × 3 = 4 × 3 = 12 m/s².
conversations.content: According to the laws of the Azeroth world, the centripetal acceleration a = 12 m/s².
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
